introduction: This presentation aims to provide insights into the competitive landscape of the North American electric vehicle market based on the 2024 IEA report. It will inform our strategic planning by identifying competitor strategies and market positioning trends.

subsections.heading: Market Dynamics: Electric Vehicle Adoption Trends
subsections.content:
• In the first quarter of 2024, a significant number of our mid-income family customer segment, equivalent to 42%, were influenced by competitor pricing and features when considering alternate EV brands.
• This customer feedback reveals an emerging shift in the competitive landscape of the EV market, impacting our market positioning.
• Competitive pricing strategies, as evidenced by the reduction in Tesla’s Model Y pricing by approximately $15,000 between mid-2022 and early 2024, are actively reshaping customer expectations and purchasing behavior.
• China's dominance in the EV market, producing over 60% of global electric cars in 2023, has intensified global competition and is pressuring automakers, including Elexion, to adjust their strategies.
• Our cross-functional teams need to consider these market dynamics in order to make informed business decisions and maintain competitiveness.
• It is critical for Elexion to align its pricing and feature offerings with market trends to prevent further customer attrition to competitors.
• Leveraging our customer feedback, we need to refine our strategies to better meet customer expectations and preferences in the evolving EV market.
subsections.justification: These points identify key market dynamics and trends shaping the EV sector, addressing the DR question by highlighting competitive strategies and market positioning.

subsections.heading: Competitor Strategies: Disruption in the EV Market
subsections.content:
• In 2023, Tesla launched its Cybertruck, a revolutionary design that diverged from traditional pickup trucks.
• General Motors announced in 2020 its commitment to launch 30 new EVs worldwide by 2025.
• Many traditional automakers have formed strategic partnerships with tech companies to develop autonomous EVs.
• Ford's Mustang Mach-E, launched in late 2020, was the company's first all-electric SUV and marked a significant shift in its EV strategy.
• Some competitors are focusing on producing EVs for niche markets, such as luxury vehicles or commercial fleets.
• Elexion's main competitors have invested heavily in R&D, with an average increase of 20% in their R&D budgets from 2020 to 2023.
subsections.justification: These points focus on past and ongoing competitor strategies, but don't provide insights into market positioning for 2026.

subsections.heading: Implications for Elexion: Navigating the EV Landscape
subsections.content:
• Elexion's high-quality EVs and user-centric technology have contributed to its strong brand reputation.
• In 2023, Elexion increased its R&D budget by 30% to develop innovative EV technologies.
• Elexion has established strategic partnerships with suppliers to ensure a stable supply of critical EV components.
• The company successfully launched two new EV models in 2023, targeting the mid-range market.
• Elexion's focus on cross-border compliance has facilitated its expansion into the Canadian market.
• The company is committed to sustainability and has implemented eco-friendly practices in its manufacturing processes.
subsections.justification: These points discuss Elexion's operations and achievements, but don't provide information on how it should navigate the market based on competitor strategies and positioning for 2026.

conclusion: The evolving competitive landscape necessitates proactive strategy adjustments to maintain our market positioning. Actionable insights have been provided for cross-functional teams to outline necessary steps towards staying competitive in the North American EV sector.
